title: What If Motivation Had a Menu?
description:
TikTok's dopamine menu trend isn't just aesthetic — it's behavioral science you can actually use. The concept comes from ADHD communities who needed external scaffolding for executive function. You list small, specific actions that give a reliable dopamine hit — not vague 'self-care'. Examples: cold water on face, 5-minute walk, texting a friend, organizing one drawer. The key is categorizing by effort: appetizers (instant), mains (15-30 min), desserts (rewards). Neuroscience backs this: predictable micro-rewards strengthen dopamine pathways better than sporadic big wins. What's your go-to appetizer?

title: Your Car's Exhaust Could Make Hydrogen
description:
Researchers developed a membrane that splits water into hydrogen using only waste heat — no electricity required. In lab tests it reached 98% efficiency at 200 °C, tapping heat from factories, power plants, and even vehicle exhaust. Current hydrogen production relies on massive power or fossil fuels. Could this turn everyday waste heat into clean fuel for your ride?

title: Laser Fusion Net Energy Gain—How?
description:
In December 2022 the National Ignition Facility fired 192 lasers at a peppercorn‑size capsule, releasing about 3.15 MJ while the lasers delivered 2.05 MJ — a net energy gain. The facility still consumes far more power overall, and scaling to a power plant requires solving repetition rate and wall‑plug efficiency. What do you think — milestone or mirage?

title: Why Your Towel Has a Hidden Stripe
description:
The stripe on a bath towel isn’t just decoration — it’s a dobby border woven tighter than the terry loops to stop fraying after many washes and to reinforce the hanging point so the towel won’t tear from the hook. This detail dates back to Ottoman hammams where towels were daily essentials and status symbols; families embroidered initials or crests on the band. Cheaper modern towels often omit it, causing faster wear. What household detail have you overlooked?
